channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US indictments reveal AI use in Russia disinformation campaign targeting 2024 election
item.description: Russia allegedly has worked to include artificial intelligence-generated and manipulated content that it passes into the mainstream along with true information to disguise it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The UK hospital where a nurse was convicted of murdering 7 babies faces investigation
item.description: An English hospital where a neonatal nurse was convicted of murdering seven babies and trying to kill seven others is facing an inquiry into how she was able to harm so many children.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley And Maria Cheng, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Francis will be only the second pope to visit Singapore, one of the wealthiest nations
item.description: SINGAPORE (AP) — When Pope Francis arrives Wednesday in Asia’s financial powerhouse Singapore for the last leg of a four-nation tour, he is expected to bring his message of unity and hope to one of the world’s richest nations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The UN chief calls the death and destruction in Gaza the worst he's seen
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. chief said Monday that the United Nations has offered to monitor any cease-fire in Gaza and demanded an end to the worst death and destruction he has seen in his more than seven-year tenure.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A robot resumes mission to retrieve a piece of melted fuel from inside a damaged Fukushima reactor
item.description: An operation to send an extendable robot into one of three damaged reactors at Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant to bring back a tiny gravel of melted fuel debris has resumed, nearly three weeks after its earlier attempt was suspended due to a technical issue.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: International manhunt underway for man accused of committing ‘most cowardly’ crime
item.description: An international manhunt is underway for a man who doused a 9-month-old baby with scalding hot coffee at a park in Australia late last month, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea's Kim vows to put his nuclear force ready for combat with US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to redouble efforts to make his nuclear force fully ready for combat with the United States and its allies, state media reported Tuesday, after the country disclosed a new platform likely designed to fire more powerful intercontinental
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: During Brazil's worst drought, wildfires rage and the Amazon River falls to a record low
item.description: Brazil is enduring its worst drought since nationwide measurements began over seven decades ago, with 59% of the country under stress — an area roughly half the size of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fabiano Maisonnave, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: National police director, fugitive banker among 9 dead in El Salvador helicopter crash
item.description: El Salvador's police director, other top-ranking officials and fugitive banker Manuel Coto were reportedly killed in a military helicopter crash Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indianapolis woman missing after rescue in Puerto Rico
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico -- Officials with the United States Coast Guard are searching for a 22-year-old woman from Indianapolis who was reported missing off a beach in San Juan, Puerto Rico. According to a news release from the coast guard, the 22-year-old was visiting the island from Indianapolis.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Elton John: Trump nickname for North Korea's Kim 'hilarious'
item.description: Rock star Elton John said former President Trump’s nickname for North Korean leader Kim Jong Un “was hilarious” in a video from Variety.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Kate, Princess of Wales, says she's completed chemotherapy and is 'looking forward to being back at work'
item.description: Kate, the Princess of Wales, says she's "finally completed" her chemotherapy treatment for cancer and is "looking forward to being back at work." "The last nine months have been incredibly tough for us as a family.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Iran threatens 'nightmare' for Israel as UN watchdog warns Tehran nuclear programs runs unchecked
item.description: Iran renews calls for a "nightmare" attack on Israel for the killing of Hamas leader Haniyeh in Tehran, Iran, as the international community watches with concern as it continues to ramp up its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah relies on 'sophisticated' tunnel system backed by Iran, North Korea in fight against Israel
item.description: Hezbollah has developed a sophisticated tunnel system with the backing of Iran and North Korea to counter Israel while simultaneously gaining support across Lebanon, becoming Israel's greatest threat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Three Israeli civilians killed at crossing between West Bank and Jordan
item.description: Three Israeli civilians were killed on Sunday by a Jordanian gunman who opened fire at the Allenby Crossing connecting the West Bank and Jordan, the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Opposition presidential candidate González flees Venezuela for asylum in Spain
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Former Venezuelan presidential candidate Edmundo González arrived in Spain on Sunday after fleeing into exile in as part of a negotiated deal with Nicolás Maduro's government that dealt a major blow to millions who placed their hopes in his opposition campaign.
item.dc:creator: JORGE RUEDA, JOSHUA GOODMAN and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An inside look at Israel's special forces rescue unit fighting terrorists, rescuing hostages: 'Full on Fauda'
item.description: An inside look at Israel's elite Yamam counter-terrorism unit; their daring Gaza hostage rescue, the unit's secretive operations, and their critical role in the global fight against terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump responds to DOJ announcement on election interference efforts: 'I don't know what it is with poor Russia'
item.description: Former President Trump dismissed findings Saturday from the Department of Justice (DOJ) about Russia's covert efforts to influence the 2024 U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: More than a dozen killed in latest Israeli strike amid polio vaccinations
item.description: Israeli strikes in Gaza killed more than a dozen people overnight Saturday, hospital and local authorities said, as health workers attempt to administer polio vaccinations amid potential rise in infections.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump or Harris? Israelis discuss presidential choice as war with Hamas, other terror groups continues
item.description: Tensions run high as the U.S. presidential elections loom near. Israelis are concerned about what the next U.S. President would mean for their nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Zealand queen ascends to Māori throne at age 27
item.description: New Zealand’s Māori king, Tūheitia Pōtatau Te Wherowhero VII, 69, has died. His daughter, Ngā wai hono i te po, 27, is the new queen and the second woman to become Māori monarch.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli forces appear to withdraw from Jenin. But the operation may not be over
item.description: JENIN REFUGEE CAMP, West Bank (AP) — Israeli forces appeared to have withdrawn from three refugee camps in the occupied West Bank by Friday morning, after a more than weeklong military operation that left dozens dead and a trail of destruction.
item.dc:creator: JALAL BWAITEL and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelensky ups pressure for more air defense systems, long-range strike capabilities
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky pushed for more air defense systems and long-range strike capabilities during a meeting Friday with the U.S. and Western leaders, making the plea as Russia continues to pound his country with missiles and drones.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Kenya school dormitory fire kills 17 students, seriously burns 13, death toll may rise
item.description: A fire in a school dormitory in Kenya has killed 17 seriously burned 13 others, according to police. The cause is still under investigation, and they fear the death toll may rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American killed in West Bank, State Department says
item.description: An American citizen has died during a protest in the West Bank, Fox News has learned. The State Department says it is aware of "the tragic death" of Aysenur Ezgi Eygi, although did not provide any more details.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says he backs Harris over Trump, adds he's ready for talks with Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he supports Vice President Kamala Harris in the 2024 race for the presidency over former President Trump after previously claiming Trump is an unpredictable politician.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why is Beijing interested in a mid-level government aide in New York State?
item.description: Linda Sun held numerous roles in New York state government, including deputy chief of staff for Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas leaders charged with crimes by Justice Department
item.description: The Justice Department is charging top Hamas leaders for their role in the October 7th massacre in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Jessi Turnure, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea launches more trash-carrying balloons across the border, South Korea says
item.description: North Korea in recent weeks has flown thousands of balloons toward the South to drop waste paper, cloth scraps and cigarette butts.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Seizure of Venezuelan leader's jet a 'clear message:' HSI agent
item.description: NewsNation learned that the U.S. has taken $2 billion in goods over the last several years from principals in Maduro's regime.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: A boat with dozens of migrants rips apart in the English Channel off France, killing 12
item.description: Most of the victims were believed to be women, some under 18, and many of the passengers didn't have life preservers, officials said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and MARK CARLSON, Associated Press, NewsNation
